completed_tasks.name: Create policy-search.json and update infrastructure (run.js, session.js)
Implement lib/search.js and wire into outlook.js

remaining_tasks.name: Live search verification against real Outlook (human checkpoint)
remaining_tasks.blocker: Chrome profile corruption must be fixed first; run.js --profile change must be reverted; snapshot parsing must be fixed

blockers.description: run.js was changed to use --profile OUTLOOK_BROWSER_PROFILE instead of --session-name. This caused agent-browser to treat the user's real Chrome Profile 1 as a user data directory, creating Profile 1/Default/ and corrupting Chrome. MUST be reverted to --session-name before any further runs.
blockers.workaround: Revert lib/run.js and lib/run.js runEval to use --session-name outlook-skill unconditionally. Remove profileArgs logic entirely.

blockers.description: extractMessageRows in lib/search.js walks snapshot.children/snapshot.nodes but agent-browser snapshot structure is flat: {data: {refs: {eN: {role, name}}}}. The tree walk finds nothing. Function needs complete rewrite to search snapshot.data.refs values.
blockers.workaround: Diagnostic code already added to search.js showing refs structure. refs role counts visible from test run. Rewrite extractMessageRows to iterate Object.values(snapshot.data.refs) looking for role=listbox by name.

blockers.description: User's Chrome Profile 1 was corrupted by --profile change. Profile 1/Default/ directory was created by agent-browser. User cannot find their working Chrome profile with bookmarks.
blockers.workaround: Separate from skill work. User needs to recover Chrome profile. Bookmarks available in snapshots: ~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Snapshots/145.0.7632.160/Profile 1/Bookmarks (96KB, Mar 2025)

human_actions_pending.action: Resolve Chrome profile situation so user has a working browser before attempting live Outlook verification
human_actions_pending.context: User's Chrome Profile 1 was corrupted when run.js was changed to pass --profile to agent-browser. Neither Chrome profile (Gabe / Your Chrome) shows correct bookmarks or pre-today history.

human_actions_pending.action: Run node outlook.js auth after run.js is reverted to --session-name, to establish a fresh skill session
human_actions_pending.context: With --session-name outlook-skill, the skill uses an isolated session. First run needs interactive auth (headed browser) to log in to myemail.accenture.com.
Tests: basic search, zero results, --limit, missing query, KQL operator. All must pass before phase 2 is complete.

decisions.decision: policy-search.json needs getbyrole in allow list
decisions.rationale: agent-browser find command uses getbyrole internally; was denied by policy during first live test
no dotenv dependency; user shouldn't need to source .env manually before every run

decisions.decision: run.js --profile change was a mistake — REVERT to --session-name
decisions.rationale: --profile with existing Chrome profile path corrupts Chrome; agent-browser treats it as user data dir not profile selector

decisions.decision: snapshot structure is flat refs dict, not nested tree
decisions.rationale: confirmed from diagnostic output: {data: {refs: {eN: {role, name}}}} — extractMessageRows must be rewritten

uncommitted_files: lib/run.js — needs REVERT of --profile change before committing
lib/search.js — diagnostic code must be removed; extractMessageRows must be rewritten for flat refs
next_action: 1. Revert run.js to --session-name (remove profileArgs logic). 2. Rewrite extractMessageRows to use snapshot.data.refs flat dict. 3. Remove diagnostic code from search.js. 4. Run node outlook.js auth to establish session. 5. Run 5 verification tests.
context_notes: Wave 1 (02-01) is fully committed and clean. All remaining uncommitted changes are debugging artifacts or fixes needed. The snapshot structure discovery is the key unresolved technical problem — refs is a flat {eN: {role, name}} dict, not a tree. We can see 'Exit search' button and multiple 'Select a message' checkboxes in refs confirming search works, but parsing needs fixing. Chrome profile issue is a side-effect of our --profile mistake and is unrelated to the core skill work.
